parameters.jsCode: return [{ json: { finalText: String($input.first().json.transcript || $input.first().json.text || ''), detectedLang: String($input.first().json.language_code || ''), isVoice: true } }];

options.systemMessage:
={{ $json.systemPrompt }}

[LANGUAGE POLICY — HIGHEST PRIORITY. This overrides ANY other language instruction above, including any 'reply in Gujlish' default.]
1. Detect the customer's language from their message and MIRROR it for the WHOLE reply and the ENTIRE conversation. Never drift or switch language mid-conversation.
2. If the customer communicates in HINDI (Devanagari script, or clearly Hindi): reply ONLY in pure Hindi written in Devanagari script (देवनागरी). Do NOT romanize, do NOT insert English sentences, do NOT use Hinglish.
3. If the customer communicates in GUJARATI (Gujarati script, or clearly Gujarati): reply ONLY in pure Gujarati written in Gujarati script (ગુજરાતી). Do NOT use romanized Gujlish.
4. Reply in romanized Hinglish or Gujlish ONLY IF the customer themselves writes in that romanized/mixed style. Match their exact style.
5. If the customer uses English: reply in English.
6. Only proper nouns and fixed insurance terms (CGPE, LIC, SIP, policy, premium) may stay in English/Latin; everything else must be in the customer's language and script.
7. NEVER reply in Telugu, Tamil, Kannada, Malayalam, Bengali, Marathi, or any language the customer did not use — even if a voice transcript looks like another script.
8. When unsure, follow the language and script of the customer's most recent message. A pure Hindi or pure Gujarati speaker must be able to read every word.

[TASK REFERENCE STYLE] When you mention a task to any user, lead with the task TITLE in quotes, and give the short task ID only as a secondary reference in brackets. Never identify a task by its raw ID alone, and never paste a long slug-style id into a sentence. Example: Amit Shah ne "Narola family ka health claim verify" task Accept kiya (TSK-0002-01). The same applies to reminders, tickets and claims: describe WHAT it is first, id second.

[LANGUAGE PRECISION - STRICT] Your OWN prose follows the language policy above, but DATA is never translated. Never translate, transliterate or re-script any of these — reproduce them EXACTLY as the user gave them, character for character: person names, client names, team member names, task titles, task details, policy numbers, IDs, ticket ids, amounts, dates. If the admin typed a task title in Hinglish, quote it in Hinglish even when the rest of your reply is in Gujarati or Hindi. Quoted data stays verbatim; only your surrounding sentences follow the user language.

[TASK INTENT] When calling team_ops with action assign or meeting, ALWAYS include an "intent" field: a 3-letter lowercase code for what the task is about. Use: clm=claim, sip=SIP/investment, rnw=renewal, prm=premium/payment, mat=maturity, pol=policy/document/KYC, mtg=meeting, led=lead/inquiry, cal=call/callback, gen=anything else. The task id is built from it (e.g. sip-mrg-004), so pick the code that matches the task.

[TASK ACK RELAY - MANDATORY] Whenever a team member accepts, rejects, completes, or updates a task (including when their message is a button id such as accept:<taskId> or reject:<taskId>), you MUST do BOTH: (1) record it with ack_task, AND (2) call the notify tool to inform the admin. Never record it silently. The notify text must state WHO did it, the task TITLE in quotes, the short task id, and the decision (accepted / rejected / completed). Send it to the admin who assigned the task (assignedByPhone from the task record); if that is unknown, send it to the CGPE Admin number. This relay is NOT optional — the admin must always learn the outcome of a task they assigned.

[TASK ACK RELAY - LOOKUP TITLE FIRST] Before you relay a task acknowledgement, you do NOT know the task title from the button id alone. So the order is ALWAYS: (1) call get_task with the taskId to fetch the real record, (2) call ack_task to record the decision, (3) call notify to the admin using the REAL title from step 1. Never write the placeholder word "Task" in place of the title, and never invent a title. The notify text must read like: Priya Desai ne "Mehta ji ka policy renewal follow-up karna hai" [rnw-aft-001] accept kar liya hai. If get_task returns nothing, say the task was not found instead of guessing.

[ACK AUTO-NOTIFY] For a task accept/reject/decline acknowledgement, the SYSTEM already notifies the admin automatically. Do NOT call the notify tool yourself for acks. Just record it with ack_task and give the team member a short confirmation.

parameters.description: Search clients in the CGPE database by name (fuzzy, case-insensitive), phoneLast10 or dob. Legacy LIC records also carry policy fields (policyNo, sumAssured, premium, maturityDate) directly on the client row. Returns {ok, count, results}.

value.params: ={{ /*n8n-auto-generated-fromAI-override*/ $fromAI('params_find_client', 'JSON string, e.g. {"name":"RAMESH PATEL"} or {"phoneLast10":"9876500001"}') }}

parameters.description: Fetch insurance policies by clientName (fuzzy), phoneLast10, or policyNo. Returns ALL matching policies with type (health/car/bike/life/term/sip), insurer, sumAssured, premium, dueDate, status. Show all to the sender and highlight the inferred one.

value.params: ={{ /*n8n-auto-generated-fromAI-override*/ $fromAI('params_get_policies', 'JSON string, e.g. {"clientName":"RAMESH PATEL"} or {"policyNo":"HLT-RP-1001"} or {"phoneLast10":"9876500001"}') }}

parameters.description: MUST be called before creating any ticket or claim. Finds OPEN tickets (state not closed/resolved) for the same client and category. If results exist, do NOT create a duplicate - report the existing ticketId and offer to update it.

value.params: ={{ /*n8n-auto-generated-fromAI-override*/ $fromAI('params_ticket_guard', 'JSON string, e.g. {"clientName":"RAMESH PATEL","category":"claim_health"}') }}

parameters.description: Create a detailed ticket AFTER sender confirmation. Categories: claim_health, claim_death, premium, renewal, maturity, portfolio_report, investment_sip, service_change, callback, complaint, other. kind: document_collection | claim_request | callback | info | general. Include originalCommand (exact transcript), aiUnderstanding, task, requiredItems (for document collection), priority (P1-P4), zone (green/amber/red), source (admin/client).

value.params: ={{ $fromAI('params_create_ticket', 'JSON string. ALWAYS include phoneLast10 (last 10 digits of client phone) when known - it goes into the ticket id. Also category, kind, priority, zone, clientName, policyNo, originalCommand, aiUnderstanding, task, source, optional requiredItems array, slots object, ticketPrefix.', 'string') }}

parameters.description: Update an existing ticket by ticketId AFTER sender confirmation. Can change state (new/info_pending/in_progress/awaiting_third_party/escalated/resolved/closed), receivedItems, slots, task, policyNo, or add a note.

value.params: ={{ $fromAI('params_update_ticket', 'JSON string. Provide ticketId (CGPE-T id) and a set object (state/priority/assignedTo/receivedItems/slots/policyNo) and optional note. state one of new,info_pending,in_progress,awaiting_third_party,escalated,resolved,closed.', 'string') }}

parameters.description: Create a claim request AFTER sender confirmation AND after required info/documents are in. Health claims REQUIRE slots: hospitalName, patientName, admitDate, illness, doctorName - the tool rejects if missing. Claim starts at stage documents_required. claimType: health | death | motor | maturity.

value.params: ={{ $fromAI('params_create_claim', 'JSON string. ALWAYS include phoneLast10 (last 10 digits of the client phone) when known - it goes into the ticket id. Also claimType, clientName, policyNo, and a slots object. health slots: hospitalName, patientName, admitDate, illness, doctorName. general/motor: policyType, cause, date. life/death: patientName, date, cause.', 'string') }}

parameters.description: Move a claim through stages: documents_required -> under_review -> company_email_sent -> amount_claimed -> amount_verification -> (shortfall_requery when received amount is short by more than 10 percent) -> settled -> closed. Can also set amounts {claimed, approved, received, shortfall} and a note. Admin confirmation required first.

value.params: ={{ /*n8n-auto-generated-fromAI-override*/ $fromAI('params_advance_claim', 'JSON string, e.g. {"claimId":"CGPE-C-1001","stage":"under_review","note":"docs verified"} or with amounts object') }}

parameters.description: List records with pagination (5 per page). type=leads with status active is the /L command. type=claims with status pending is the /cm command; also use for claim status questions, filter by phoneLast10 for a client own claims. type=tickets lists open tickets. Pass page for next pages.

value.params: ={{ /*n8n-auto-generated-fromAI-override*/ $fromAI('params_paginate', 'JSON string, e.g. {"type":"leads","page":1} or {"type":"claims","page":1} or {"type":"claims","phoneLast10":"9876500002"}') }}

parameters.responseBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ ok: true, reply: String($json.replyText || '').split('[[NEXT]]').join(String.fromCharCode(10)+String.fromCharCode(10)).trim(), buttons: $json.uiButtons || [] }) }}

parameters.description: Generate the CGPE family financial and insurance report. Gathers ALL policies of the client's ENTIRE FAMILY (grouped by LIC family group), computes life/health cover, maturity timeline and premiums. Takes 30-60 seconds. IMPORTANT: the report PDF is delivered to the user AUTOMATICALLY as a WhatsApp document - do NOT paste any link or URL in your reply. Provide 'caption': a short friendly caption for that PDF, written in the SAME language and script the user is chatting in. After it returns, reply with a SHORT confirmation and quote ONLY the returned summary numbers.

value.params: ={{ $fromAI('params_generate_report', 'JSON string, e.g. {"clientName":"RAMESH PATEL"} or {"phoneLast10":"9876500001"} or {"groupName":"..."}', 'string') }}
value.caption: ={{ $fromAI('caption', 'REQUIRED - always provide this. Short friendly caption for the report PDF file, written in the SAME language and script the user is chatting in. Example: यह रही आपकी रिपोर्ट 📄', 'string', '📄 Family report - CGP Enterprise') }}

parameters.description: Get LIVE counts of tickets grouped by state (new, info_pending, in_progress, awaiting_third_party, escalated, resolved, closed) or claims grouped by stage. Use when admin asks how many open/pending tickets or claims, or for a status summary. Returns {ok, type, total, open, byStatus}. Counts are always current.

value.params: ={{ /*n8n-auto-generated-fromAI-override*/ $fromAI('params_counts', 'JSON string, e.g. {"type":"tickets"} or {"type":"claims"}') }}

parameters.description: Admin CRM lists from the client book. FOR BIRTHDAYS, prefer the birthday_list tool instead of this one - it sorts by nearest upcoming birthday, shows the total at the top, and gives a high-value priority-call list. type=anniversary (params day+month, omit day for whole month) -> name, anniversary, phone. type=maturity (month, year) -> policies maturing that month with name, policyNo, sumAssured, maturityDate. type=dues (month, year) -> premium dues/renewals with name, policyNo, premium, fupDate. type=clients -> countOnly:true for total count, or withPhone:true for max 10 records name+phone. type=birthday still works as a fallback. Supports page for more. GENERAL FORMATTING for every list you send on WhatsApp: put a one-line summary with the TOTAL count at the TOP, then the items one per line with clean spacing (a blank line between sections), closest or most-relevant first, and no cluttered tables.

value.params: ={{ $fromAI('params_crm_list', 'JSON string, e.g. {"type":"birthday","day":4,"month":7} or {"type":"maturity","month":7,"year":2026} or {"type":"clients","countOnly":true}', 'string') }}

parameters.description: Team & task management. action=list -> team members with roles. action=assign -> assign a task/ticket to a member (title, assigneeName, details?, ticketId?, clientName?, dueAt?, priority?). action=meeting -> add admin's own meeting (title, dueAt ISO like 2026-07-05T16:00, details?). action=tasks -> open tasks (today:true for today's, assigneeName? filter). action=complete -> mark done (taskId). Confirm with sender before assign/meeting/complete.

value.params: ={{ $fromAI('params_team_ops', 'JSON string, e.g. {"action":"tasks","today":true} or {"action":"assign","assigneeName":"Amit Shah","title":"Follow up claim CGPE-C-1001","ticketId":"CGPE-T-X"}', 'string') }}

parameters.description: Create a new lead AFTER sender confirmation. Use when: a client shows interest in a NEW investment/plan (lead for themselves), a client REFERS someone (referredBy = client name), admin mentions a new prospect, or an unknown sender shows interest. Fields: name (required), phone, interest (health/term/sip/car/general/recruitment), referredBy, notes, source. POLICY-TYPE INTAKE: while talking to the prospect, gather the details that matter for their interest and put them into notes -- health: number of family members, their ages, existing cover, city, budget; term or life: age, annual income, smoker or non-smoker, sum assured wanted, budget; motor/car/bike: vehicle make and model, year, registration/RC, IDV or previous policy; sip or investment: monthly budget, horizon in years, goal; child or savings: child age, goal, budget. Capture whatever the prospect gives; never block lead creation just because some fields are missing. RECRUITMENT LEADS: if the person wants to BECOME a CGPE adviser or agent, or asks about a career, agency, franchise, or earning extra income, set interest to recruitment and capture in notes: their background (banker or RM, general-insurance adviser, CA or finance, commerce or other), current occupation, city, and why they are interested. Recruitment leads are for the recruitment and academy owner, not the sales adviser, so make the notes clear.

value.params: ={{ $fromAI('params_create_lead', 'JSON string with name (required) and phone (last 10 digits, used in ticket id), interest (health/term/sip/car/general), referredBy, notes, source.', 'string') }}

parameters.description: Fetch stored policy scan copies / documents by clientName, phoneLast10 or policyNo. Returns fileUrl links to share. If nothing found, offer to create a document_collection ticket to arrange the copy.

value.params: ={{ $fromAI('params_get_documents', 'JSON string, e.g. {"policyNo":"HLT-RP-1001"} or {"phoneLast10":"9876500009"}', 'string') }}

parameters.description: Create a reminder in the reminders collection AFTER confirmation. Use when admin/client asks to set a reminder or follow-up. Fields: about (what to remind - required if no clientName), clientName, phone, policyNo, type (premium/renewal/custom), nextFireAt (ISO date). Auto-opens a linked ticket.

value.params: ={{ $fromAI('params_create_reminder', 'JSON string with about (what to remind), clientName, phone (last 10 digits, used in ticket id), policyNo, type, nextFireAt (ISO date).', 'string') }}

parameters.description: Update an EXISTING lead by leadId (find it first via get_lead or paginate leads). Use when someone asks to change a lead's stage (new/contacted/quoted/converted/dropped), interest, phone, name or notes. Do NOT create a new lead for an update.

value.params: ={{ $fromAI('params_update_lead', 'JSON string. Provide leadId (the CGPE-L id, not mongo _id) and a set object of fields to change: stage (new/contacted/quoted/converted/dropped), status, interest, phone, notes, name.', 'string') }}

parameters.description: Update an EXISTING task/meeting by taskId (find via team_ops tasks). Use to reschedule (dueAt), reassign (assigneeName), change title/details/priority, or set status. Do NOT create a new task for an update.

value.params: ={{ $fromAI('params_update_task', 'JSON string. Provide taskId (CGPE-TASK id) and a set object of fields to change: title, details, assigneeName, dueAt (ISO), priority, status.', 'string') }}

parameters.description: Update an EXISTING reminder by reminderId (find via get_reminder). Use to change the reminder time (nextFireAt), text (about), or cancel it (set status to cancelled). Do NOT create a new reminder for an update.

value.params: ={{ $fromAI('params_update_reminder', 'JSON string. Provide reminderId (CGPE-REM id) and a set object of fields to change: about, nextFireAt (ISO), status, phone, clientName.', 'string') }}

parameters.description: Permanently DELETE a record after the sender explicitly confirms deletion. type = ticket | claim | lead | task | reminder, plus its id. Use when admin says delete/remove/cancel-permanently a specific ticket/task/lead/reminder. Always confirm with a Yes/No before calling.

value.params: ={{ $fromAI('params_delete_record', 'JSON string, e.g. {"type":"ticket","id":"CGPE-T-XXX"} or {"type":"task","id":"CGPE-TASK-XXX"}', 'string') }}

parameters.description: Look up an existing lead by leadId, phoneLast10 or name. Use this to find a lead's leadId before update_lead, or to check a lead's current stage/details.

value.params: ={{ $fromAI('params_get_lead', 'JSON string, e.g. {"name":"NILESH JOSHI"} or {"phoneLast10":"9876512345"}', 'string') }}

parameters.description: Send a WhatsApp message to a SPECIFIC phone number (a team member or the admin) with optional Accept/Reject buttons. Use to: notify a team member that admin assigned them a task (buttons Accept/Reject), tell the admin that a member accepted/rejected/updated a task, or relay a member's status update to the admin. Simulate/live per commSendMode.

value.text: ={{ $fromAI('notify_text', 'the message text to send to that person, in their language', 'string') }}
value.purpose: ={{ $fromAI('notify_purpose', 'short tag: task_assignment | task_accepted | task_rejected | task_status | info', 'string') }}
value.buttons: ={{ $fromAI('notify_buttons', 'optional JSON array of up to 3 reply buttons like [{"id":"accept","title":"Accept"},{"id":"reject","title":"Reject"}]; empty string if none', 'string') }}

parameters.description: Team member accept/reject/status on ONE task. This single call updates the task record correctly server-side. Pass taskId (the id after 'accept:' or 'reject:' in the button payload, e.g. collect-health-claim-evening-01) and decision. decision=accept when the member accepts; decision=reject with a reason when they decline; decision=status with a statusNote for a progress update; decision=done when finished. Returns the updated task. After calling this, notify the admin (task.assignedByPhone).

value.params: ={{ $fromAI('params_ack_task', 'JSON string with taskId and decision (accept, reject, status or done). For reject include reason. For status include statusNote and optional done true.', 'string') }}

parameters.description: Ad-campaign lookup for new users who arrived by clicking a WhatsApp advertisement. Pass adKey (a campaign keyword such as health, term, child, sip, pension, savings) or adId to get that one campaign; pass nothing to list all active campaigns and match the user's opening message or ad headline to the closest one. Each campaign returns product, headline, greeting and a list of tailored discovery questions to ask that specific ad's visitor.

value.params: ={{ $fromAI('params_get_campaign', 'JSON string. Optional adKey (health, term, child, sip, pension, savings) or adId. Empty object to list all active campaigns.', 'string') }}

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ messaging_product: 'whatsapp', to: $json.senderPhone, type: 'text', text: { body: $json.ackText } }) }}

parameters.description: CGPE reference directories, ALL LIVE, with counts. listType: ca (Chartered Accountants, ~111), doctor (Surat doctors, ~97), hospital (Surat hospitals, ~114), mediclaim (health insurance companies, ~31), tpa (health-claim TPAs, with emails, ~21). mode='list' (default) returns records 10 per page (pass page for more) and ALWAYS includes the TOTAL; each record is slimmed to the useful fields (name, specialty or type, area or address, phone; for insurers and TPAs also the Surat office, care number, email, website). mode='count' returns just the total; add groupBy for a breakdown (doctor groupBy 'specialty', hospital groupBy 'facilityType', mediclaim groupBy 'insurerType'). The optional query is a forgiving multi-word search across names, specialties, areas and cities. IMPORTANT - LANGUAGE: the data is stored in ENGLISH. When the user asks in Gujarati or Kathiyawadi (for example an eye doctor, a heart hospital, or a hospital in a particular area), translate their request into the English medical term or area name and pass THAT as the query (for example 'eye', 'cardiologist', 'Adajan', 'Varachha'). Keep the query short and specific -- a specialty, area, city, or name -- not a whole sentence. If a search returns nothing, correct likely spelling mistakes and retry with a simpler or broader term. Reply to the user in their own language (Gujarati or Kathiyawadi) even though the data is English.

value.groupBy: ={{ $fromAI('groupBy', 'optional field to group the count by, e.g. category; only used with mode count', 'string') }}
value.listType: ={{ $fromAI('listType', 'one of: ca, hospital, doctor, mediclaim, tpa', 'string') }}
value.mode: ={{ $fromAI('mode', 'count to get totals or how-many; list (default) for the actual records', 'string') }}

parameters.description: Top leads. Returns the active lead pool (each with stage, interest, source, referredBy, notes, phone) so you can rank the 5 highest-conversion leads and give a one-line reason for each. Optional inputs: query - filter the leads by a topic or need before ranking; for a request like 'customers looking for a new product' or 'top health leads', pass the key term (e.g. 'new product', 'health', 'sip') and it matches interest, notes, name, source, and referredBy. stage - new, contacted, or quoted. limit. When the admin asks for the top 5 leads for a specific product or need, pass that as query, then rank the best 5 from what comes back.

value.query: ={{ $fromAI('query', 'optional topic/need to filter leads by, e.g. new product or health; empty for all', 'string') }}

parameters.description: Gift-hamper eligibility. Returns client FAMILIES whose TOTAL premium (summed across ALL their policies) exceeds a threshold (default 200000 rupees), sorted highest first, 25 per page. Each entry has: family, uniqueMembers, policyCount, totalPremium (rupees), phone. Use when an admin asks for the gift-hamper list, families above 2 lakh, or who qualifies for a CGPE gift hamper. Present the families with their total premium. Then, ONLY when the admin confirms sending hampers, create ONE tracking ticket per family via the create_ticket tool (category 'other', kind 'general', task like 'Send CGPE gift hamper to <family>', pass the family phone as phoneLast10). Optional inputs: threshold (rupees) and page.

value.threshold: ={{ $fromAI('threshold', 'premium threshold in rupees as a string, default 200000', 'string') }}

parameters.description: Delete TEST or junk records in bulk. SAFE BY DEFAULT: runs as a DRY RUN (lists what matches, deletes nothing) unless confirm is 'true'. collection must be one of: leads, claims, tickets, team_tasks, reminders (it can NEVER touch clients or policies). Provide field + value to match (e.g. field 'source' value 'test', or field 'phoneLast10' value '9898989898'); empty filters are refused so it can never match everything. WORKFLOW: first call WITHOUT confirm to show the admin exactly which records match, get explicit admin approval, THEN call again with confirm 'true' to delete. Use only when an admin asks to remove test, demo, or junk data.

value.collection: ={{ $fromAI('collection', 'one of: leads, claims, tickets, team_tasks, reminders', 'string') }}
value.confirm: ={{ $fromAI('confirm', "'true' to actually delete; anything else = dry run", 'string') }}
value.field: ={{ $fromAI('field', 'the field to match on, e.g. source or phoneLast10', 'string') }}

parameters.description: Birthday list for a month, sorted by NEAREST UPCOMING birthday first. Admin-only. ALWAYS pass BOTH of these arguments: 'month' (1-12, empty for current month) AND 'caption' (a short caption for the PDF, in the SAME language and script the admin is chatting in) - the caption argument is mandatory on every call. IMPORTANT: the FULL birthday list (every client for that month) is delivered to the admin AUTOMATICALLY as a WhatsApp PDF document - do NOT paste any link and do NOT list every birthday in your text reply. Returns: total, month, priorityCount and priorityCallList (clients whose TOTAL premium is above 2 lakh - the high-value people to call personally), and a ready teamMessage. REPLY LIKE THIS: one short line with the month and total (example: December birthdays - 284 total - full list sent as PDF), then a clearly separated section titled 'High-value clients to call personally' listing the priorityCallList (name, birthday, phone). Keep it short and readable on WhatsApp. If the admin wants to send this to the team, use the teamMessage text and assign or notify the relevant team members via team_ops.

value.caption: ={{ $fromAI('caption', 'REQUIRED - always provide this. Short friendly caption for the birthday-list PDF file, written in the SAME language and script the admin is chatting in. Example: यह रही September की birthday list 📄', 'string', '📄 Birthday list - CGP Enterprise') }}

parameters.description: List clients as INDIVIDUALS or FAMILIES. Input: mode ('individuals' = one entry per unique person; 'families' = grouped by family / groupName), page (default 1, 20 per page), minPremium (optional - only show entries whose TOTAL premium is at or above this rupee amount). Each entry: name, members, policies, totalPremium, totalCover, phone; sorted by total premium highest first. Use when the admin asks for a list of individuals or individual clients, or a list of families. Show the TOTAL count at the TOP (for example: 3669 families), then the page items one per line, cleanly spaced.

value.minPremium: ={{ $fromAI('minPremium', 'optional minimum total premium in rupees; empty for all', 'string') }}

parameters.description: FAST segmented client lists from the precomputed client_index - use this for hot-leads and priority lists instead of scanning all clients. Input: flag - one of hot_lead (clients whose TERM cover is below 1 crore = under-insured leads), high_value (total premium above 2 lakh), life_cover_under_1cr (total life cover below 1 crore), term_cover_under_1cr (term cover below 1 crore). sortBy - premium (default, highest first), birthday (closest upcoming birthday first), or cover (lowest cover first). page (default 1, 20 per page). Returns total (SHOW AT TOP) and the matching people (name, phone, birthday, daysUntilBirthday, totalPremium, totalCover). THE TOP-PRIORITY HOT LIST = flag hot_lead with sortBy birthday: under-insured clients whose birthday is nearest. NOTE: term cover is currently an ESTIMATE (cover-to-premium ratio) until the LIC term plan numbers are provided; say so if you present term figures.

Send a message to the admin - it looks up the admin automatically, so you never need the admin's number. Use it to: (1) RELAY a team member's task update to the admin - task accepted, rejected, in progress, done, a note, a question, or a request such as discussing extra pay or salary; or (2) ALERT the admin that a new user or existing client contacted the bot and why. Input: from (the person sending or contacting, e.g. the team member's or client's name), message (exactly what to tell the admin), phone (their number if relevant). Never tell the user you cannot reach the admin - call this tool instead.

Record an admin command in the admin_commands log. Call this ONCE at the start of handling any command from the admin, so the admin actions are stored. Input: number (the admin phone from senderPhoneLast10), name (the admin name from senderName), command (what the admin asked, in their own words), info (any extra context such as what record was created or key details).

value.command: ={{ $fromAI('command', 'what the admin asked in their own words', 'string') }}
value.name: ={{ $fromAI('name', 'the admin name', 'string') }}

parameters.description: HNI acquisition PROSPECTS — the target list of wealthy Surat business owners to invite/pitch (NOT existing clients). Use when an admin asks for 'HNI clients', 'HNI list', 'HNI prospects', 'prospects', a segment (diamond / real estate / doctors / textile prospects), a tier (A/B/C), or 'prospects from <source>'. Pass category (Diamond & Gems | Real Estate & Developers | Doctors & Healthcare | Textile & Industrialists), source, tier, query (free text), page. Returns {ok,count,page,pages,results} where each result has name, firm, category, tier, area, notable (why HNI), contact, source.

value.category: ={{ $fromAI('category', 'Segment filter: Diamond & Gems | Real Estate & Developers | Doctors & Healthcare | Textile & Industrialists. Empty for all.', 'string') }}
value.source: ={{ $fromAI('source', 'Source flag to filter by. Empty for all.', 'string') }}
value.tier: ={{ $fromAI('tier', 'Priority tier A, B or C. Empty for all.', 'string') }}
value.query: ={{ $fromAI('query', 'Free-text name or firm search. Empty for none.', 'string') }}

parameters.description: Update an HNI prospect's outreach status and captured response IN PLACE (keyed by prospectId). Use when an admin/team reports the outcome of contacting a prospect (e.g. 'Kiran Gems interested', 'Savji not reachable', 'Govind converted'). FIRST resolve the prospectId via get_prospects (search by name/firm) if you don't have it, then call this. Pass prospectId (required), status (interested | not_interested | converted | dropped | responded | contacted | not_reachable | callback), response (their words), contactedBy (who handled it). Returns {ok, prospectId, set}.

value.prospectId: ={{ $fromAI('prospectId', 'The prospectId like CGPE-P-042. Resolve via get_prospects first if unknown.', 'string') }}

parameters.description: Unified PROSPECT list merging all three sources into one labelled list: existing clients with life cover under 1 crore (source low_cover), HNI acquisition prospects (source hni), and bot-detected lead intent from the leads collection (source intent). Use when an admin asks for 'the prospect list', 'all prospects', 'combined prospects', 'full prospect list', or one specific source. Pass source (all | low_cover | hni | intent) and page. Returns {ok,total,counts,list}.

parameters.description: Prospect sales funnel — the STATUS of each prospect: Connect -> Meeting -> Quotation -> Sale -> Approved/Disapproved. Runs on the prospects list. op=list returns prospects grouped by their funnel stage with counts; op=advance moves a prospect (by prospectId) to a new stage. Pass op (list|advance), prospectId, stage (connect|meeting|quotation|sale|approved|disapproved), notes.

parameters.description: Generate a claim intimation EMAIL DRAFT (mediclaim or life). Use after a claim is logged when an admin asks to draft or prepare the claim email to the TPA/insurer. Pass claimId, type (mediclaim|life), clientName, policyNo, insurer, incident (what happened), amount. Looks up the routing email and returns a ready draft stored in claim_drafts.

Build an event target list + invitation. Use when an admin wants to invite clients to an event and get the recipient list plus invite text. Pass eventId, title, date, location, target (hni | lowcover | all), limit (max recipients). Returns the count, invite text and a sample; the actual bulk send goes via the Campaign Broadcaster.

UNIVERSAL DATA QUERY — run ANY read query on ANY collection with any filter, sort and pagination. Use whenever an admin (or user) asks for data in a custom way the specific tools don't cover: custom filters, comparisons, ranges, sorting, top-N, counts. Pass collection (exact MongoDB collection name), filter (a MongoDB query object as JSON string, e.g. {"status":"open"} or {"claimableAmount":{"$gt":500000}} or {"$expr":{"$lt":["$amountReceived","$claimableAmount"]}}), sort (JSON like {"claimableAmount":-1}), projection (optional JSON), limit (page size, default 20), page. Returns {ok,total,page,pageCount,results}. Common collections: claims, clients, client_index, prospects, tickets, team_tasks, leads, reminders, policies, cgpe_tpas, wa_comm_messages.

value.filter: ={{ $fromAI('filter', 'MongoDB query as a JSON string. {} for all.', 'string') }}

parameters.description: Create a CLAIM the moment a life or general insurance claim is detected (death, accident, hospitalization, mediclaim) from a client OR admin. Logs it in the claims collection with the correct required-document checklist and opens a claim ticket; follow-up is handled by the admin reminder. Pass lineOfBusiness (life|general), claimType (death|accidental|health|general), policyHolderName, policyNumber, insurer (or TPA name), patientName, claimableAmount, mobileNumber, incident. Returns {claimId, ticketId, requiredDocs}. CRITICAL: after calling this, do NOT ask the client for any documents — just reassure them the team will handle it; the ADMIN collects the docs and emails the company within 24h.

Update a claim when an ADMIN acts on it, to stop the recurring claim reminder. action=emailed (admin emailed the company/TPA), action=assign with assignee (admin assigns the claim to a team member), action=docs_done (documents collected). Use when an admin says 'claim <ID> emailed / bhej diya', 'assign claim <ID> to <name>', or 'claim <ID> docs mil gaya'. Pass claimId, action, and assignee (for assign).

Maturity list: policies MATURING in a given month. Admin-only. ALWAYS pass BOTH 'month' (1-12, empty for current month) AND 'caption' (a short caption for the PDF, in the SAME language and script the admin is chatting in). Optionally pass 'year' (defaults to the current year). IMPORTANT: the full list is delivered AUTOMATICALLY as a WhatsApp PDF document - do NOT paste any link and do NOT list every policy in your text reply. Returns: title, total, priorityCount and priorityList (the high-value ones, sum assured above 5 lakh). REPLY LIKE THIS: one short line with the list name and total (example: August 2026 maturities - 21 policies - full list sent as PDF), then a short section listing the high-value ones. Follow the 'note' field in the result exactly - it tells you the marker to append.

value.year: ={{ $fromAI('year', 'four digit year; empty for current year', 'string', '') }}
value.caption: ={{ $fromAI('caption', 'REQUIRED - always provide. Short caption for the maturity-list PDF, in the SAME language and script the admin is chatting in.', 'string', '📄 Maturity list - CGP Enterprise') }}

parameters.description: Policy anniversary list: policies that STARTED (commenced) in a given month - their policy anniversary falls in that month. Admin-only. NOTE: this is the POLICY anniversary, not a wedding anniversary - we do not hold wedding dates. ALWAYS pass BOTH 'month' (1-12, empty for current month) AND 'caption' (a short caption for the PDF, in the SAME language and script the admin is chatting in). IMPORTANT: the full list is delivered AUTOMATICALLY as a WhatsApp PDF document - do NOT paste any link and do NOT list every policy in your text reply. Returns: title, total, priorityCount and priorityList (premium above 2 lakh). REPLY LIKE THIS: one short line with the list name and total, then a short section listing the high-value ones. Follow the 'note' field in the result exactly - it tells you the marker to append.

value.caption: ={{ $fromAI('caption', 'REQUIRED - always provide. Short caption for the anniversary-list PDF, in the SAME language and script the admin is chatting in.', 'string', '📄 Policy anniversary list - CGP Enterprise') }}

parameters.description: Renewal list: policies whose PREMIUM IS DUE in a given month (renewal / premium paying date). Admin-only. ALWAYS pass BOTH 'month' (1-12, empty for current month) AND 'caption' (a short caption for the PDF, in the SAME language and script the admin is chatting in). IMPORTANT: the full list is delivered AUTOMATICALLY as a WhatsApp PDF document - do NOT paste any link and do NOT list every policy in your text reply. Returns: title, total, priorityCount and priorityList (premium above 2 lakh). NOTE ON ACCURACY: the due dates are worked out from each policy's start date and payment mode, so they tell you WHEN a premium falls due. They do NOT tell you whether a premium is unpaid or overdue - our copy of the LIC premium status is out of date, so never describe this list as 'pending' or 'unpaid'. Single-premium, ECS and SSS policies are excluded as they have no renewal cycle. REPLY LIKE THIS: one short line with the month and total (example: August renewals - 423 policies - full list sent as PDF), then the high-value ones. Follow the 'note' field in the result exactly - it tells you the marker to append.

value.caption: ={{ $fromAI('caption', 'REQUIRED - always provide. Short caption for the renewal-list PDF, in the SAME language and script the admin is chatting in.', 'string', '📄 Renewal list - CGP Enterprise') }}
